# @ativie/ripple

Official TypeScript SDK for Ripple — self-hosted real-time event tracking.

## Installation

```bash
npm install @ativie/ripple
```

## Usage

```ts
import Ripple from '@ativie/ripple';

const ripple = new Ripple({
  apiKey: 'rp_your_api_key',
  baseUrl: 'https://your-ripple-instance.com',
});

await ripple.track({
  channel: 'payments',
  event: 'Payment Failed',
  user: 'user_123',
  metadata: {
    amount: 5000,
    reason: 'insufficient_funds',
  },
});
```

## API

### `new Ripple(options)`

| Option | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `apiKey` | `string` | ✓ | Your Ripple API key (`rp_...`) |
| `baseUrl` | `string` | ✓ | URL of your deployed Ripple backend |
| `timeout` | `number` | | Request timeout in ms (default: `10000`) |
| `retries` | `number` | | Retries on transient errors (default: `2`) |

### `ripple.track(options)`

| Option | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `channel` | `string` | ✓ | Channel name (max 100 chars) |
| `event` | `string` | ✓ | Event name (max 200 chars) |
| `user` | `string` | | User identifier (max 100 chars) |
| `metadata` | `object` | | Arbitrary key/value data |
| `timestamp` | `Date` | | Defaults to now |

### `ripple.identify(userId, metadata?)`

Shorthand that tracks a `User Identified` event on the `users` channel.

## Error handling

```ts
import Ripple, { RippleError } from '@ativie/ripple';

try {
  await ripple.track({ channel: 'signups', event: 'New User' });
} catch (err) {
  if (err instanceof RippleError) {
    console.error(err.message, err.statusCode);
  }
}
```
